title: Jayson Tatum won’t play in Celtics’ penultimate regular season matchup
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum is out due to right Achilles repair management for the Celtics' game against the Pelicans.
Jaylen Brown will play despite managing left Achilles tendinitis; he averages 31.8 points in recent games.
Boston Celtics aim to secure the No. 2 seed in the Eastern Conference, holding a 54-26 record.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her daughter’s courage through cancer treatment
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Lori Robertson, 49, from Concord, will run the 2026 Boston Marathon to honor her daughter's cancer journey.
Her daughter Emma was diagnosed with a medulloblastoma that metastasized, requiring complex treatment over the past year.
Robertson's marathon run aims to celebrate Emma's bravery and the support from Massachusetts General Hospital staff.

title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens won't debut for the Bruins against the Tampa Bay Lightning on Saturday.
Coach Marco Sturm opts for patience with Hagens, emphasizing acclimation to the team's system.
The Bruins can clinch a playoff spot with a win against Tampa, their first postseason since missing last year.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list includes Boston billionaires Noubar Afeyan, Bill Cummings, and Herb Chambers.
Historical figures like Benjamin Franklin and Babe Ruth, with ties to Boston, were featured on Forbes' historic self-made list.
Forbes' rankings are based on a Self-Made Score evaluating financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 2026 Boston Marathon will take place on April 20 with 30,000 participants from over 130 countries and all 50 U.S. states.
This year introduces six starting waves, with Wave 1 beginning at 10 a.m. and Wave 6 at 11:21 a.m.
Live coverage of the marathon will be available on WCVB Channel 5 and the Very Local app starting at 4 a.m.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Patriots hire former Giants and Titans defensive coordinator Shane Bowen as an analyst.
Shane Bowen, 39, has previous coaching experience under Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at Titans and Texans.
Bowen strengthens Patriots' defensive staff, joining Zak Kuhr and cancer-recovered Terrell Williams in key roles.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston offers public restroom access through libraries, community centers, firehouses, and some retail establishments across the city.
The Age Strong Commission provides an interactive map of public restrooms, emphasizing their importance for public health.
State law allows individuals with medical conditions access to employee-only restrooms in retail stores under certain conditions.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins, a Masconomet Regional High School freshman, alleges inadequate response to harassment, including sexual violence threats.
Teachers Erin O’Brien and Carol Lund claim administrators ignored discrimination reports against the school's golf coach and team.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes claims of inaction, citing privacy laws and the district's response protocols.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts residents spent over $137 million extra on gas since the Iran conflict began in February 2026.
Average gas prices rose from under $2 to over $4 a gallon, increasing pump spending by 35% since late February.
Massachusetts home heating oil costs rose 37.9% year-over-year, adding $224 per oil tank since March 2025.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Massachusetts campgrounds offer availability for World Cup fans with sites near Boston Stadium in Foxborough.
Canoe River Campground, 7 miles from Boston Stadium, offers weekday and weekend stays ranging from $51 to $187.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres, 40 miles away, has glamping and RV sites available, with prices from $140 per night.
